content.verdict: The Farm at Cape Kidnappers occupies 6,000 acres of working sheep and cattle station above the Pacific Ocean in Hawke's Bay, with a Tom Doak-designed clifftop golf course consistently ranked among the world's top 50 and a main lodge of 22 suites set among rolling pasture. Sister property to Kauri Cliffs and Matakauri (Robertson Lodges), Cape Kidnappers blends working-farm authenticity with refined hospitality and adds the bonus of Hawke's Bay's celebrated wine country to the honeymoon equation.
content.best_room: The Suites in the cottage clusters across the property have private terraces, gas fireplaces, and views over the working farm, the golf course, or the ocean. Cliff-top Suites with Pacific views are the honeymoon priority. The Owner's Cottage is a four-bedroom freestanding villa with private pool. For couples, an ocean-view Suite delivers the property's defining outlook.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Hawke's Bay Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Napier-Hastings Airport (1 hour from Auckland), then a 30-minute drive through Hawke's Bay vineyards to the lodge. Pre-dinner drinks on the main lodge terrace looking across the working farm to the Pacific. Dinner with Hawke's Bay produce and the region's exceptional Bordeaux-style red blends.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cape Kidnappers Golf
itinerary_7_nights.description: Tom Doak's Cape Kidnappers course is a clifftop dramatic — fairways run out toward the Pacific on narrow ridge-fingers, and the par-3 sixteenth plays across a yawning canyon. A round for golfers; non-golfers can take a guided tour of the course in the late afternoon when the light is best. Spa and pool in the afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Hawke's Bay Wineries
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hawke's Bay is New Zealand's premier red-wine region — full-day tour through Craggy Range, Te Mata Estate, Black Barn, and Elephant Hill, with lunch at a vineyard restaurant. The region's Syrah and Cabernet-Merlot blends are among the southern hemisphere's finest.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Gannet Colony Visit
itinerary_7_nights.description: Cape Kidnappers is home to the world's largest mainland gannet colony — a 4WD farm tour from the lodge across the working station to the cape's tip, where thousands of birds nest on the cliff edge from October to April. The drive itself is an extraordinary trip across the property's rolling sheep country.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Napier Art Deco & Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 30 minutes to Napier — the Hawke's Bay city famously rebuilt in Art Deco style after the 1931 earthquake, now one of the world's most concentrated Art Deco urban environments. Walking tour, lunch on the seafront, and afternoon at Cape Coast vineyards on the way back.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Horseback Ride & Spa Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: A morning horseback ride across the working farm with one of the lodge's resident guides — through pasture, native bush gullies, and out to clifftop viewpoints over the Pacific. Afternoon at the lodge's spa for couples treatments before the final tasting menu dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Round or Wineries & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final round on the Cape Kidnappers course or one more wine country morning before the transfer back to Napier-Hastings for the connection to Auckland. Many honeymooners pair Cape Kidnappers with the South Island for a complete two-island honeymoon.

caveats: Cape Kidnappers is golf-centric — like Kauri Cliffs, the course is a major draw and the property's culture reflects it; non-golfers should embrace the country-club ambience.
The property is a genuine working farm — the lodge sits among 6,000 acres of pasture; couples expecting manicured resort grounds should know that the experience embraces farm authenticity.
Beach access requires a long 4WD descent — there is no walk-in beach from the lodge; the cliffs are spectacular but the ocean is for viewing rather than swimming.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Cape Kidnappers is a thoughtful honeymoon choice for couples who want to combine luxury lodge hospitality with Hawke's Bay's wine country and a working-farm setting. The clifftop suites, gannet colony, and proximity to Napier's Art Deco character give the stay a distinctively New Zealand sense of place.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: October through April is the warm season — gannet colony active, vineyards open, golf course in best condition. December-February is summer peak with long evenings. May-September is cooler with vineyard pruning, log fires, and lower rates.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Cape Kidnappers books out 4-6 months ahead for peak summer and golf weeks. The Owner's Cottage requires longer lead times. The lodge accepts approximately 50 guests across all suites.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Cape Kidnappers is not adults-only — children are welcomed in the cottage suites and Owner's Cottage, and the working-farm setting is family-friendly. The main lodge atmosphere skews adult.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Cliff-top ocean-view Suites are the romantic honeymoon choice — private terrace, fireplace, and the Pacific view that defines the property. The cottage layout gives each suite the feel of a private retreat.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Napier-Hastings Airport is 30 minutes from the lodge, with direct flights from Auckland (1 hour). Auckland International Airport is the international gateway. Helicopter transfers from Auckland are available for scenic arrivals over the volcanic plateau and Hawke's Bay.
